name: El Bierzo – circular between Pobladura de las Regueras and Folgoso de la Ribera
name_original: El Bierzo – circular entre Pobladura de las Regueras y Folgoso de la Ribera
region_intro: Touring cycling around Igüeña offers routes through the mountainous El Bierzo Alto region of León, Spain. The area features significant elevation changes, diverse forests of pines, oaks, and hollies, and river valleys carved by the Boeza and Tremor. Historical elements, such as ancient Roman gold exploitations and sections of the Camino Olvidado, are also present along the routes. This terrain provides varied conditions for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Igüeña The most popular touring cycling route is El Bierzo – circular between Pobladura de las Regueras and Folgoso de la Ribera, a 26.8 miles (43.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 17 minutes to complete. This difficult route navigates mountainous terrain with notable elevation gains. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Comarca de Omaña – circular from Montrondo through the Omaña Valley and the Valle Chico, a moderate 17.7 miles (28.5 km) path. This route explores the Omaña Valley, offering views of the natural landscape. Local touring cyclists also love the Quintana del Castillo y San Feliz de las Lavanderas — circular por Astorga, a 22.7 miles (36.5 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 49 minutes. Touring cycling around Igüeña is defined by mountainous terrain, diverse forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The routes in Igüeña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 50 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Igüeña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Igüeña?
A: Based on komoot, there are &lt;strong&gt;12 touring cycling routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Igüeña. These routes offer a variety of experiences, from moderate to difficult, navigating the mountainous El Bierzo Alto region.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level for touring cycling routes in Igüeña?
A: Touring cycling routes around Igüeña are generally characterized by &lt;strong&gt;mountainous terrain with significant elevation changes&lt;/strong&gt;. Most routes are classified as difficult (9 out of 12), with a few moderate options available (3 out of 12). Expect climbs and descents that reward you with stunning vistas.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Igüeña?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Igüeña are circular. For example, you can explore the Omaña Valley on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037750977/comarca-de-omana-circular-from-montrondo-through-the-omana-valley-and-the-valle-chico" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Comarca de Omaña – circular from Montrondo through the Omaña Valley and the Valle Chico&lt;/a&gt;, or experience the varied landscapes of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363131256/quintana-del-castillo-y-san-feliz-de-las-lavanderas-circular-por-astorga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quintana del Castillo y San Feliz de las Lavanderas — circular por Astorga&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter diverse forests of pines, oaks, hollies, and yews. Rivers like the Boeza and Tremor carve through the landscape, forming natural pools. Notable waterfalls include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gualta Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5555148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Paseo de los Molinos&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, routes may offer glimpses of peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5555072" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Nevadín (2,077 m)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there historical sites or attractions to explore while cycling in Igüeña?
A: Absolutely. Igüeña has a rich history. You can find remnants of ancient Roman gold exploitations, particularly around the Tremor River. The historical "Camino Olvidado" (Forgotten Way), a medieval branch of the Camino de Santiago, also passes through the area. Cyclists can visit sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5518472" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Campo de Martín Moro Toledano&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1657319" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Folgoso Dam&lt;/a&gt;, an old hydroelectric power station, which is featured on routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40386042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Folgoso Dam – Folgoso de la Ribera loop from Cabanillas de San Justo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Igüeña?
A: The region's diverse forests are particularly vibrant during autumn, offering picturesque routes. While specific weather patterns vary, spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures for cycling. Summer can be warm, but a fluvial beach in Igüeña offers a refreshing break.

Q: What types of cycling surfaces can I expect on touring routes in Igüeña?
A: Given the mountainous terrain and historical context, you can expect a mix of surfaces. While specific details for each route vary, the area is known for its diverse landscapes, suggesting a combination of paved roads, gravel paths, and potentially some unpaved sections, typical for touring in a rural, mountainous region.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Igüeña?
A: While many routes in Igüeña are classified as difficult due to significant elevation changes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037750977/comarca-de-omana-circular-from-montrondo-through-the-omana-valley-and-the-valle-chico" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Comarca de Omaña – circular from Montrondo through the Omaña Valley and the Valle Chico&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 17.7-mile (28.5 km) path that explores the Omaña Valley, offering a less strenuous option for families with some cycling experience.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Igüeña?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the challenging mountainous terrain, the diverse forests, and the opportunity to combine physical activity with the exploration of natural and historical sites. Over 50 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Igüeña's varied terrain.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging touring routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, Igüeña offers several challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037730628/el-bierzo-circular-between-pobladura-de-las-regueras-and-folgoso-de-la-ribera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Bierzo – circular between Pobladura de las Regueras and Folgoso de la Ribera&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 26.8-mile (43.1 km) trail with notable elevation gains. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158915" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tremor de Arriba – Tremor de Arriba loop from Pobladura de las Regueras&lt;/a&gt;, covering over 50 km with significant climbing.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation along the routes?
A: While specific amenities on each route are not detailed, the region has small villages and towns. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer or more remote routes. Researching local cafes, pubs, or accommodation in towns like Igüeña or nearby settlements before your ride is recommended for planned stops.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the cycling routes in Igüeña?
A: Information on specific public transport options for cyclists to Igüeña is limited. It's generally recommended to plan your journey by car, especially if you are bringing your own touring bicycle. Parking is typically available in the starting villages of the routes, such as Pobladura de las Regueras or Montrondo.
tips: In this town you can learn about what the shepherds did with the Vecera de las Mañías, a herd of cows that did not give milk. There is even a hiking route on this subject, in case you want to combine your cycling route with a different walk.

Pretty town with stone houses and interesting festivals, such as a medieval one or another dedicated to botillo, the traditional food of the area (in other towns there is also a festival). In his church there is an altarpiece older than the building, so it was probably brought from another temple.
